checks.a_analysis: The HTML output faithfully reproduces the single-column visual organization of the source PDF. The document structure, including the cover page, table of contents, and main body sections, follows the same logical flow as the original. Content sections such as 'Strategic Direction for the Council for Data Driven Collaboration' maintain their grouped layout using tables, which is an appropriate conversion of the boxed content in the PDF. The transition from a fixed-page layout to a responsive HTML view does not confuse meaning or disrupt the visual intent.

The HTML version successfully renders all tables from the source PDF. This includes the complex Strategic Direction tables (found on pages 19-21 of the PDF), Table 1 'Required attributes for Seamless Parcels' (page 36), and Table 2 'Outreach presentations' (page 45). The HTML tables feature clear row and column separation, correct header alignment, and readable content. No misaligned columns or overlapping text are visible.

All meaningful images, including the decorative cover map and the thirty-two figures documenting various GIS projects and meetings, are present in the HTML output. The conversion has correctly identified and rendered photos (e.g., Figure 1), diagrams (e.g., Figure 3), and dashboard screenshots (e.g., Figure 11). There are no broken image icons or missing meaningful graphics.

No body content (paragraphs, tables, or figures) is duplicated in the HTML output. The appearance of headings in the navigation Table of Contents as well as the main body is an intentional and acceptable accessibility feature and does not constitute a defect.
